purpose: This repository contains end-to-end testing infrastructure for Cross River Bank's lending and selling services, simulating the entire lending environment to validate loan processing from origination through purchase, including interest calculations, fee processing, and account transfers.
businessFeatures: Volume fee processing and true-up calculations
Loan type grooming and investor changes

apis.purpose: Get account configurations for loan actions
Get accounts for loan type changes
Get account balance for a standard account
Get account balance for a sub-account

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Represents a loan with attributes like loan_id, mpl_id, issuing_bank_id, loan_type, loan_amount, interest_rate, etc.

dataEntities.name: Contract
dataEntities.description: Defines the terms of lending between partners, including fees, interest calculation methods, and timing

dataEntities.name: Batch
dataEntities.description: Represents a group of loans being processed together during purchase

dataEntities.name: Transfer
dataEntities.description: Represents a financial transaction between accounts

dataEntities.name: LoanAction
dataEntities.description: Represents actions performed on loans such as purchase or fee collection

dataEntities.name: AccountConfig
dataEntities.description: Configuration of accounts used for various financial operations

messagingPatterns.name: SQS Payment Queue
messagingPatterns.description: Asynchronous messaging for payment processing from COS simulator to web-api

messagingPatterns.name: RabbitMQ Hooks
messagingPatterns.description: Event-driven notifications for loan status changes, batch completions, and other important system events

messagingPatterns.name: Transfer Outbox
messagingPatterns.description: Outbox pattern implementation for reliable transfer processing

findings.title: Suboptimal transaction monitoring
findings.description: The code uses polling patterns with hardcoded sleep intervals to monitor transaction completion, which could be inefficient and fragile. The application would benefit from an event-driven approach to reduce resource consumption and improve reliability.

findings.title: Manual service orchestration
findings.description: The tests rely on manual orchestration of multiple dependent services with fragile timing assumptions. Implementing a proper service coordination pattern with health checks would improve test stability and reliability.

ambiguities: The exact contract structure between MPLs (Marketplace Lenders) and the bank isn't fully defined in the code
The criteria for determining when a loan is ready for purchase are not fully documented
The relationship between the test repository and actual production systems isn't clear
The complete workflow for loan servicing and how servicing data updates affect loan status
